title: Lethargy and Hypothermia - Neonatal Sepsis
chief_complaint: 5-day-old full-term infant with lethargy, poor feeding, temperature instability (hypothermia 35.8°C), and grunting. Mother had prolonged rupture of membranes (28 hours).

physical_exam.general: Lethargic, mottled skin, capillary refill 4 seconds, weak cry

hidden_data.csf: WBC: 350, Protein: 150, Glucose: 30, Gram stain: Gram-positive cocci

key_learning_points: GBS = most common cause of early-onset sepsis
Intrapartum antibiotic prophylaxis for GBS+ mothers

patient_responses.pregnancy: Mother was GBS positive, did not receive antibiotics before delivery

hints.risk_factors: GBS+ mother without prophylaxis + prolonged ROM

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Neonatal sepsis with meningitis - this is a neonatal emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is neonatal sepsis. The risk factors + leukopenia + positive cultures confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Neonatal sepsis: Ampicillin 100mg/kg + Gentamicin 4mg/kg or Cefotaxime for meningitis. Supportive care: maintain temperature, fluids, respiratory support. Duration: 14-21 days for meningitis. Good outcome with prompt treatment.
